subject: Computer Networking
title: Tim Berners-Lee Invents the Web
description: A fast breakdown of Tim Berners-Lee Invents the Web for builders.

hook.text: In 1989 at CERN, a physicist wanted a better way to share documents. He invented the World Wide Web.

buildup.text: Tim Berners-Lee created three things: HTML for pages, HTTP for transfer, and URLs for addressing.

discovery.text: His first web page went live on August 6, 1991. It explained what the web was and how to use it.

twist.text: CERN made the web free for everyone. If they had patented it, the web might have become a paid service.

climax.text: Within five years, the web went from a physics tool to the most popular service on the internet.

punchline.text: One physicist's side project became the world's information platform.

quiz.question: What three technologies did Tim Berners-Lee create for the web?
